module_mood_contract: practitioner fluency open — 'I've come a long way; the frontier is still open.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood lands per phase + close — 8+/10 at every teaching beat.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Single-phase lecture. Phase 1 mood_score: 9. Close mood_score: 9. No beat below 8. The brevity is the discipline; recurrence of M1's two questions lands as earned recognition at M6 opener. Mood-floor exemption not needed — this is a teaching beat (recognition-via-recurrence), and it scores 9.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood doesn't resolve early — M6 practitioner-fluency not short-circuited.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The two frontiers are left open. Maintainer block watch-for: 'Don't answer the two frontiers.' No resolution in the body. The lecture withholds; M6 exercise delivers the earned encode step.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teaching moment lands reliably across reasonable persona-skill variation.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Teaching moment: the recurrence — same two questions from M1, now felt differently because the student has five modules behind them. Lands for any student who attended M1 (recognition). Lands for M1-skippers as two well-posed open questions that frame the session horizon. Neither persona path can skip the echo; the three-sentence body has no detour.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Big Idea fidelity — module Big Idea honored or mood contract served.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This is a ~2 min opener lecture declared as 'does not teach a new term' (maintainer block). Its role is not to re-state the Big Idea but to prime the practitioner-fluency mood and hand off to 'Story of Module 6.' The Big Idea (two-run gap-diagnosis + skill-authoring) belongs to the exercise; this opener honors the arc-closure mood contract by naming the still-open horizon. No fidelity gap.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: File's close hands off to the next file's open.
rules_evaluated.evidence: spot-gaps-build-the-loop.md line 29-31: The 2 frontiers → Story of Module 6 → Exercise. The two open questions prime the Story lecture's permission-giving beat (strategy doc: 'Permission-giving, not credibility-performance. Goes first because permission works before the work'). Handoff verified against module file structure.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Body teaches the work; trainer manages the room — no authored conversation cues.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Three sentences of lecture prose. No pair/share, no pause cues, no room-management language. Fully clean.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture with no fenced prompts. No forcing function expected or present.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers.
rules_evaluated.evidence: This lecture is a horizon-setter / ceiling-opener (shows the still-open frontiers), not a meta-frame closer (which would name a pattern the student just lived through). Per check_lectures.md rule 1: 'Demo lectures showing a ceiling go at the beginning.' The recurrence of two open questions functions as a permission-granting ceiling-setter for M6, placed first in the module — correct position. The meta-frame rule does not fire on ceiling-setters.
M6 is practitioner-fluency mood, not a contrast-mood module (M5-style). Rule 2 fires on contrast-mood modules only.
The two frontier questions frame forward-looking curiosity and not-yet-knowing. No shame, no correction of what the student did. Epistemic unease is the open horizon, not having-done-wrong.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lecture opens with 'We have come a long way' (positive arc acknowledgment) followed by two forward-looking questions. No failure-mode framing, no deficit language.
